<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines standard sources using a direct HTTPS download from the official GNU FTP server: `https://ftp.gnu.org/pub/gnu/ncurses/ncurses-$pkgver.tar.gz`. The PGP signature is included and validated using a known and trusted key (fingerprint `19882D92DDA4C400C22C0D56CC2AF4472167BE03` belonging to upstream maintainer Thomas Dickey). The `sha256sums` array uses `'SKIP'` for the signature file, which is standard practice for VCS or signed files that are verified via PGP rather than hash checksums.

The global scope of the PKGBUILD contains only declarative metadata (e.g., pkgname, pkgver, source, etc.) and no executable commands. There is no command substitution, no remote code execution, and no network activity triggered outside of the declared source fetch. The `pkgver()` function is absent, so no dynamic versioning logic is executed during sourcing. Therefore, running `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` will safely download the declared source and verify its PGP signature without executing any untrusted code.

While the `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` functions contain logic that modifies build directories and installs libraries, these are irrelevant under the current narrow scope because `--noprepare` and `--nobuild` prevent their execution. The risk of malicious behavior during this phase is nonexistent.
</details>
<summary>
Only safe, standard source fetch and declarations
</summary>
</security_assessment>

There are no network requests, obfuscated commands, or unexpected file operations. All actions are consistent with standard Arch Linux packaging practices and serve the stated purpose of providing ABI-5-compatible ncurses libraries. No malicious or suspicious behavior is present.
